solution:
def detectCapitalUse(self, word: str) -> bool:
    first_upper = word[0].isupper()
    for i in range(1, len(word)):
        if word[i].isupper() != first_upper:
            return False
    return True

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution checks if the first character is uppercase and then iterates through the rest of the word. If the case of any character differs from the first character, it immediately returns False. Otherwise, it returns True, indicating that the usage of capitals in the word is correct. This solution has a linear time complexity (O(n)) and constant space complexity (O(1)), making it efficient for solving the problem.
